items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation:
Vague doubts haunt me once again...
Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Translation is identical to the approved/reference version. It preserves the mock-heroic register of «терзают смутные сомнения» and keeps the clipped parallelism of «У Шпака — …, у посла — …», which is crucial for the comedic callback. No issues.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Translation is identical to the approved/reference version. It keeps the repetitive, banal politeness that creates the era-clash humor (Soviet social cliché vs. royal setting) and reads naturally as drunken over-introduction. No issues.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Translation is identical to the approved/reference version. It preserves the performative, authoritarian punch of «требую» and the comic escalation of Bunsha’s role-play. No issues.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Translation is identical to the approved/reference version. It maintains the archaic/high-register melancholy and the blunt address «старушка», which drives the humor in the modern setting. No issues.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Translation is identical to the approved/reference version. It correctly keeps “demons” as the agent and preserves the panicked, incantatory repetition that sells the misunderstanding of technology as sorcery. No issues.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Translation is identical to the approved/reference version. It retains the elevated, religious proclamation and the comic contrast with Soviet/technical reality. No issues.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Translation is identical to the approved/reference version. It captures the MC/announcer vibe and the collective-release energy of the moment, functioning as a natural English equivalent rather than a flat literal “Everyone dances!”. No issues.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Translation is identical to the approved/reference version. It keeps the breezy self-satisfaction and opportunistic tone (a burglar delighted by “luck”), and reads idiomatically in English. No issues.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Translation is identical to the approved/reference version. It correctly avoids calling the eggplant spread “caviar” in English (which would confuse), while preserving the punchline structure and the satirical inversion of luxury vs. Soviet everyday food. No issues.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Translation is identical to the approved/reference version. It preserves the comedic inventory rhythm, the self-important “domestic” qualifier, and the trailing “Three” that signals Shpak’s escalating/exaggerating tally. No issues.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Translation is identical to the approved/reference version. It keeps the calm, sing-song fatalism and the shared-absurdity punch of the line, which is central to the humor. No issues.

summary.interpretation: 0.00 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.00 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
